fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer audiologist license to new mexico, audiologist compact new mexico, new mexico audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathology, Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensing Practices Board
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department
board.mailing_address:
New Mexico Regulation & Licensing Department
P.O. Box 25101
Santa Fe, NM 87504

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination administered by the Praxis Examination Program

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in New Mexico.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Application
steps.description: Navigate to the RLD Professional & Occupational Licensing Portal at https://rld.nm.gov/, create an account, log in, and select the Audiologist initial license application for the New Mexico Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology, Audiology, and Hearing Aid Dispensing.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the online application form within the RLD Professional & Occupational Licensing Portal and pay the $100 initial licensure fee.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official submission of all required supporting documents including official transcripts verifying your degree, official Praxis examination scores from ETS, verification of your ASHA Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC-A), and verification of supervised clinical experience if applicable.

steps.title: Complete New Mexico Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Access and complete the open-book New Mexico Jurisprudence Examination, which can be found on the board's 'Applications & Forms' page, and submit proof of completion as instructed.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided within the RLD Professional & Occupational Licensing Portal or on the board's website to complete a criminal background check (CBC) via fingerprinting.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in New Mexico
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in New Mexico, utilize the RLD Professional & Occupational Licensing Portal for initial application submission.

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Navigate to the RLD Professional & Occupational Licensing Portal at https://rld.nm.gov/. 2. Click on the 'Login / Create Account' link located in the top right corner. 3. Select 'Register for a new account' and follow the prompts to provide your personal details, create a username and password, and complete the registration process. 4. Once registered, log in to the portal to begin your audiologist license application.
